system.description: Mind Over Matter shock trooper with extreme physical enhancement, martial training, and unstable psionic side effects.
system.notes: Play the character as a high-risk combatant whose body and mind are both being pushed past human limits.

occSkills.name: Acrobatics
occSkills.notes: Apply the class bonus to Acrobatics rolls and balance checks during play.

grantedAbilities.name: M.O.M. Conversion
grantedAbilities.notes: Roll the class attribute and endurance bonuses when the character is created. The warrior can stay awake for three days, needs only four hours of sleep, and ignores pain until reduced to 10 hit points or less.

grantedAbilities.name: Bio-Regeneration
grantedAbilities.notes: By dropping into a helpless meditative trance, the character restores 2D6 hit points and 3D6 S.D.C. in 2D4 minutes. A full six hours of trance restores all S.D.C. and 4D6 hit points.

grantedAbilities.name: Crazy Hero Tables
grantedAbilities.notes: At higher levels, roll the escalating mental side effects called out in the class description and play them forward as part of the character's decline.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Start with Hand to Hand: Martial Arts. Evil characters may replace it with Hand to Hand: Assassin.

unresolvedNotes: Roll the listed starting S.D.C., hit point, P.S., P.P., P.E., and Spd increases during character creation, including the stated minimum attributes.
Roll 6D6 P.P.E. and 6D6 plus M.E. for starting I.S.P.
The class gets one extra W.P. at level three in addition to the two skill picks shown in the level-up flow.
Enhanced senses, saving throw bonuses, and the delicate-touch bonus should still be played from the class text during sessions.

system.description: Knight-errant wilderness champion with a code of chivalry, martial training, and the legendary Psi-Sword.
system.notes: Play the code of chivalry at the table. The class is built around honor, restraint, and protecting the innocent.

occSkills.name: Language
occSkills.notes: The character speaks American and Dragonese/Elven at 96% and chooses two more languages at +30%.

occSkills.name: Gymnastics
occSkills.notes: Apply the class bonus to Gymnastics rolls and balance checks during play.

grantedAbilities.name: Psi-Sword
grantedAbilities.notes: Summon the knight's psionic blade as described in the class text. At first level it inflicts 1D6 M.D. and gains another 1D6 M.D. at levels 3, 6, 9, 12, and 15.

grantedAbilities.name: Code of Chivalry
grantedAbilities.notes: Never attack an unarmed foe, never strike from behind, honor your word, and defend the weak even when it costs you.

grantedAbilities.name: Potential Psionics
grantedAbilities.notes: Most cyber-knights awaken psychic gifts. If the character is one of them, choose three powers from the Cyber-Knight list and track I.S.P. by the class text.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Start with Hand to Hand: Martial Arts.

unresolvedNotes: Roll 1D4 for M.A., M.E., P.S., P.E., and Spd, plus 1D4x10 S.D.C. during character creation.
The 80% chance of psionic powers is not enforced automatically. Use the Cyber-Knight psionic list manually if the character qualifies.
At least two of the starting related skills must come from Physical and another two must be W.P. choices.

system.description: Elite Glitter Boy power armor pilot trained to fight in one of the most famous war machines in the world.
system.notes: Treat the Glitter Boy suit as the character's signature vehicle and use its heavy weapon loadout and support systems during play.

occSkills.name: Robot Combat: Elite
occSkills.notes: This training is specific to Glitter Boy power armor.

grantedAbilities.name: Glitter Boy Training
grantedAbilities.notes: The character is fully trained to pilot Glitter Boy power armor, brace for the Boom Gun, and handle the armor's battlefield systems.

grantedAbilities.name: Boom Gun Familiarity
grantedAbilities.notes: Use the Glitter Boy's main rail gun, recoil anchors, and ammunition supply exactly as the armor entry describes during combat.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Start with Hand to Hand: Basic. The pilot may trade up to Expert or Martial Arts by paying the normal skill cost from the class text.

unresolvedNotes: The class assumes access to Glitter Boy power armor even though a dedicated Glitter Boy armor item is not yet in the imported catalog.
Choose either First Aid or Paramedic as the final O.C.C. medical skill at level one.

system.description: Hardened mercenary tracker loaded with weapons, fieldcraft, and black-market augmentation.
system.notes: Run the character as a professional gunfighter and hunter who leans on gear, tracking, and cybernetic edge instead of institutional support.

grantedAbilities.name: Mercenary Loadout
grantedAbilities.notes: A headhunter starts with a full battlefield kit, multiple weapon slots, and enough field gear to operate independently for weeks.

grantedAbilities.name: Built for the Hunt
grantedAbilities.notes: Lean into tracking, ambush sense, concealment spotting, and augmentation choices when planning missions and bounty work.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Start with Hand to Hand: Expert. The mercenary may upgrade to Martial Arts or Assassin by spending one related skill.

unresolvedNotes: Roll 1D4 cybernetics during character creation. Then choose one bionic appendage and two bionic weapons for that limb from the imported augmentation catalog.
The current level-up flow does not support the variable 1D4 starting cybernetic count automatically.

system.description: Chemically enhanced super-soldier who lives at impossible speed and pays for it with a shortened lifespan.
system.notes: Play the character as someone who can do almost anything in a fight right now, but who is burning through tomorrow to buy that power.

grantedAbilities.name: Juicer Enhancement Suite
grantedAbilities.notes: Roll the class S.D.C., hit point, P.S., P.E., P.P., and Spd increases during character creation and apply the listed minimum attributes from the class text.

grantedAbilities.name: Automatic Defensive Reactions
grantedAbilities.notes: The Juicer gets a dodge or parry against every incoming attack, even from behind or surprise, as long as the character is conscious and free to move.

grantedAbilities.name: Juicer Detox
grantedAbilities.notes: If the player wants to try to beat the countdown, use the detoxification rules exactly as written in the class description.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Start with Hand to Hand: Expert. The character may upgrade to Martial Arts or Assassin by spending one related skill.

unresolvedNotes: The current actor model does not enforce the Juicer lifespan, drug dependency, or detox sequence automatically.
Related Pilot skills only gain the class bonus on military pilot types in the source text; that distinction is still manual during play.
Roll starting money and black-market goods from the class text during character creation.
